title: Mate with Bishop and Knight
description: Centralize your pieces, drive the king to the edge, use the knight's W maneuver to force it toward the bishop-colored corner, and complete the mating net.

variants.label: Light-squared bishop
variants.detail: White: king, bishop and knight vs king

solution.pgn:
[SetUp "1"]
[FEN "8/8/8/8/8/4k3/8/2N1KB2 w - - 0 1"]
[Result "1-0"]

1. Bg2 Kd4 2. Kd2 Ke5 3. Ke3 Kf5 4. Nd3 Kg5 5. Be4 Kf6 6. Kd4 Ke6 7. Kc5 Ke7 8. Kd5 Kf6 9. Kd6 Kf7 10. Ke5 Kg7 11. Ke6 Kg8 12. Ne5 Kf8 13. Kf6 Kg8 14. Nf7 Kf8 15. Bh7 Ke8 16. Ne5 Kd8 17. Ke6 Kc7 18. Nd7 Kb7 19. Bd3 Kc6 20. Bc4 Kc7 21. Bb5 Kd8 22. Nf6 Kc7 23. Nd5+ Kd8 24. Kd6 Kc8 25. Ke7 Kb8 26. Kd8 Kb7 27. Kd7 Kb8 28. Ba6 Ka7 29. Bc8 Kb8 30. Nb4 Ka7 31. Kc7 Ka8 32. Bb7+ Ka7 33. Nc6# 1-0
solution.explanation: White first centralizes the king and coordinates the bishop and knight to create a wall that drives the black king to the edge. Black heads toward the wrong corner, where a light-squared bishop cannot deliver mate. White then begins the W maneuver: the knight travels through e5, f7, e5, d7, f6, d5 and b4 while the king and bishop continually rebuild the barrier. Once Black is permanently confined near the light-squared a8 corner, White arranges the final net. Bb7+ forces the king to a7, and Nc6# completes the mate.
